title: The Beginning
description: Mike, Carol, and Andrea Lago open their first ice cream stand at 71 Lafayette Road in Rye, NH. Arnold "Gramps" Wade teaches them the craft of homemade ice cream, and Steve and Andrea travel to the prestigious Penn State Ice Cream School to master the science behind it.

title: Kahlua Fudge Brownie is Born
description: Steve Grenier creates the legendary Kahlua Fudge Brownie — a recipe that would earn the title of New England's Best Flavor five years later.

title: New England's Best Flavor
description: WBZ TV4 Boston awards Kahlua Fudge Brownie "New England's Best Flavor" — putting Lago's on the regional map.

title: Cappuccino Slam Debuts
description: The beloved Cappuccino Slam — espresso ice cream with homemade fudge hazelnut swirl — becomes an instant classic.

title: First NH Magazine Win
description: New Hampshire Magazine names Lago's "NH's Best Ice Cream" for the first time — the start of a historic run.

title: Olympic Bronze & Frappe Fame
description: Nephew Scotty Lago wins Olympic bronze in halfpipe snowboarding in Vancouver — the Scotty Lago's Bronze Run flavor celebrates his triumph. NECN also names Lago's Best Frappe in New England.

title: Albanian Baklava & 30 Years
description: The unique Albanian Baklava flavor is introduced. Lago's celebrates 30 years serving the NH Seacoast with four generations of family involved.

title: Funky Panda is Born
description: Mike Lago creates what becomes the #1 best seller: Funky Panda — oreo ice cream with toasted coconut and caramel swirl.

title: 40th Anniversary Season
description: Lago's celebrates four decades of handcrafted ice cream on the NH Seacoast.

title: Wall Street Journal & Yankee Magazine
description: Featured in both the Wall Street Journal and Yankee Magazine — national recognition for a beloved local institution.

title: 13× NH's Best Ice Cream
description: New Hampshire Magazine names Lago's "NH's Best Ice Cream" for the 13th time, cementing a legacy unmatched in the state.
